title: S1: context ranks critical patterns by relevance — with DISCRIMINATION and conservation, not survival
action: Per G5, hardened by G10 and G11 after the advisor measured that the naive design would ship a path sort wearing a relevance label. MEASURED STARTING POINT: the okf-migration-f2 work item carries NO bee.areas; only 2 of 50 patterns carry any; tag overlap against its five tags hits 1 of 50 — so 49 patterns tie at score 0. A tag-overlap-only ranking is therefore NOT acceptable however green it looks. (1) DESIGN THE SIGNAL BY MEASURING FIRST, not by guessing: before implementing, hand-label the ~50 critical patterns as relevant / irrelevant for the okf-migration-f2 work item (a migration/coverage/knowledge task) and record the labels in the trace. Then measure which signals actually separate them — candidate signals include tag overlap, area overlap, token overlap between the pattern body and the work item body/description, overlap with the work item's required_context concepts, and recency. Report the separation each signal achieves. Pick the combination that discriminates and SAY WHY, quoting the numbers. (2) IMPLEMENT ranking + cut, with a small guaranteed FLOOR of highest-scoring patterns so a genuinely universal lesson is never evicted. (3) CONSERVATION AND AUDIT (G11): the payload gains an `excluded` array of {path, score, reason} for every critical pattern not in entries; assert entries + truncated + excluded == the full critical set; emit `zero_signal_count` and FAIL the run when it exceeds a pinned threshold — a ranking where most items score zero is not a ranking. (4) TESTS (G10) — the acceptance is DISCRIMINATION: a fixture with hand-labelled relevant and irrelevant patterns must produce NON-TIED scores with every labelled-relevant pattern outranking every labelled-irrelevant one; a universal pattern survives a tiny budget via the floor; ties break deterministically; conservation holds; zero_signal_count over threshold fails. EXPLICITLY REJECTED as an oracle: 'the 5 relevant entries of the live manifest survive' — those are the work item, its plan and required_context at ranks 1-3 and the cut is a prefix cut, so that test passes even if every pattern were dropped. Do not write it. RED-FIRST: all of (4) captured red before implementing. (5) Re-run the live manifest and report the before/after entry composition.

truths: red evidence: the discrimination tests failed before implementation (verbatim)
the hand-labelled relevant/irrelevant set is recorded in the trace WITH the per-signal separation numbers that drove the design choice
discrimination proven: every labelled-relevant pattern outranks every labelled-irrelevant one, scores non-tied
conservation proven: entries + truncated + excluded == the full critical set, asserted in a test
zero_signal_count is emitted and an over-threshold run FAILS — demonstrated
the live okf-migration-f2 manifest before/after composition quoted: how many of the 40 previously-included irrelevant patterns are now excluded, and what the freed budget went to
prohibitions: never ship a ranking where most items tie at zero — that is a path sort wearing a relevance label
never write the rejected survival oracle (the 5 top-ranked entries are prefix-protected and prove nothing)
never silently drop a critical pattern — every exclusion is named with its score and reason (G11)
no new runtime dependency; bundle-only reads (D23)
do not touch bee-scribing in this cell — S2 owns the write half

trace.outcome: context ranks critical patterns by measured relevance (AUC 0.805 vs 0.550 for tag overlap), cuts to 20 with a 3-pattern floor, and names every exclusion with its score; live manifest 45 entries/19726 tokens -> 25/12651, 0 truncated, 27 excluded, conservation 20+2+27=49
deviations: Three pinned D27 assertions were updated rather than worked around, because the floor and the audit fields are genuine behavior changes: the manifest key set widened; a critical's reason now carries its relevance score and rank; and the tight-budget test now asserts the floor as the one named exception to the prefix cut. The two strongest invariants were deliberately KEPT: total_est never exceeds budget, and a zero budget still includes nothing.

The floor reserves its cost out of the budget remaining AFTER rank 1, not out of the whole budget. First implementation let the floor displace the work item under a tight budget; a manifest without its work item is not a manifest.

CRITICAL_RELEVANCE.KEEP pinned at 20, not 12: measured on the live bundle, K=20 keeps 6 of the 8 labelled-relevant criticals that reach the block while dropping 27 of 47; K=12 keeps only 4 for a further ~1.7k tokens. Ranks 16-20 are the densest relevant band after the top 5.

HONEST LIMIT, reported not hidden: the shipped signal does NOT achieve total separation on the live data (AUC 0.805 over all 49, 0.756 over the 47 that reach the block). Two labelled-relevant patterns fall below the cut and are named in `excluded` with their scores: 20260712-enumerated-move-trap-in-migration-cells.md (0.098, rank 30 — a very short body, so its own vocabulary is thin) and 20260715-shipping-a-lib-file-means-shipping-the-manifest.md (0.133, rank 26). Total separation is asserted on the fixture; on live data the claim is a measured 0.805, not a proof.

The freed live budget went nowhere new: the okf-migration-f2 work item carries no bee.areas, so no area decisions rank below the critical block. The 7.1k tokens are simply not spent — total_est 19726 -> 12651 — rather than reallocated.

The rejected survival oracle ('the 5 relevant live entries survive') was NOT written.",
  "conservation": "test_knowledge.mjs 'knowledge context CONSERVES the critical set' at budgets 100000/2500/900/0: entries + truncated + excluded accounts for every bee.critical exactly once, and critical_total states the population. buildContextManifest THROWS a typed conservation error if any critical is unaccounted. Live: 20 kept + 2 via required_context + 27 excluded = 49 of 49.",
  "zero_signal": "test_knowledge.mjs 'FAILS when zero_signal_count exceeds the pinned threshold': a 12-critical bundle with disjoint vocabulary exits 1 with typed zero_signal naming 12 of 12; the companion check proves the guard is inert below ZERO_SIGNAL_MIN_POPULATION=10 while still reporting the count.",
  "floor": "test_knowledge.mjs 'FLOOR': with a required_context chain that costs more than the floor, a budget of work-item + floor keeps all 3 floor criticals and truncates the higher-ranked required_context areas; total_est <= budget; the work item is never displaced by its own floor; a zero budget still includes nothing.",
  "determinism": "tied scores order by path; two runs byte-identical.",
  "live_before_after": "okf-migration-f2 at budget 20000 \u2014 BEFORE: 45 entries, 40 critical patterns, 7 truncated, 19726 est tokens. AFTER: 25 entries, 20 critical patterns, 0 truncated, 27 excluded (each with score + rank reason), 12651 est tokens, zero_signal_count 0, floor 3. 27 of the 47 competing patterns are now named-and-excluded rather than dumped into the payload; the 7 previously-truncated criticals are now excluded by score instead of by path order; ~7.1k tokens freed.",
  "verify": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS run_verify: 75 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=60678ms (exit 0)",
  "red_first": "7 new checks captured red before implementation: DISCRIMINATES, relevance CUT + excluded, CONSERVES, FLOOR, tie determinism, zero_signal FAILS, zero-signal floor inert, real-cut. Verbatim errors: 'the payload must carry an excluded array, got undefined'; 'the payload must NAME the floor it guarantees, got undefined'; 'an all-zero ranking must FAIL the run, got status 0'; 'the count is still REPORTED below the floor, got undefined'; '26 criticals exist in this fixture, got undefined'.",
  "red_failure_evidence": "BEFORE (git-show of the prior behavior): .bee/bin/lib/knowledge.mjs step (4) was `for (const concept of concepts) { if (beeOf(concept.data).critical === true) select(concept.path, 'critical pattern'); }` \u2014 include EVERY critical, path-sorted, no score, no cut, no excluded array. Measured on the live bundle at budget 20000 that produced 45 entries of which 40 were critical patterns (13,000 of 19,726 est tokens), plus 7 further criticals silently truncated by PATH ORDER, so an irrelevant pattern could evict a relevant one. PRE-CHANGE CHECKS THAT FAILED (captured red before any implementation, verbatim): 'FAIL knowledge context: the relevance CUT keeps the top-scoring criticals and NAMES every excluded one with score and reason (G11) \u2014 Error: the payload must carry an excluded array, got undefined'; 'FAIL knowledge context FLOOR ... \u2014 Error: the payload must NAME the floor it guarantees, got undefined'; 'FAIL knowledge context FAILS when zero_signal_count exceeds the pinned threshold ... \u2014 Error: an all-zero ranking must FAIL the run, got status 0'; 'FAIL knowledge context: the zero-signal guard is inert below the pinned population floor \u2014 Error: the count is still REPORTED below the floor, got undefined'; 'FAIL knowledge context: the relevance cut is a real cut \u2014 Error: 26 criticals exist in this fixture, got undefined'; 'FAIL knowledge context DISCRIMINATES ...'; 'FAIL knowledge context CONSERVES the critical set ...' \u2014 54 passed, 7 failed. After implementation the same file reports 61 passed, 0 failed, and three PREVIOUSLY GREEN pinned D27 assertions went red first (manifest key set; \"critical pattern\" reason equality; the pure-prefix tight-budget cut) \u2014 the direct proof that shipped behavior changed."
}
